title: euryomyrtus recurva
intro: Euryomyrtus recurva, also known as the lemon myrtle, is a small to medium-sized shrub native to Australia, belonging to the myrtle family (Myrtaceae). The plant is rich in essential oils, particularly citral and limonene, which possess antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, making it useful for various medicinal purposes. The health benefits of Euryomyrtus recurva include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce stress and anxiety, and provide relief from cold and flu symptoms. Euryomyrtus recurva can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and essential oil blends, offering a natural remedy for various health concerns.
